messages.content: reescribeme esta frase con otras palabras

Vuelve a escribir esta oración utilizando sinónimos u otras palabras similares:

"El creativo diseño del nuevo edificio sorprendió a todos." 

"La innovadora estructura del recién construido edificio impresionó a cada persona presente."
DMF has been used as a solvent in the perfume and pharmaceutical industries for a long time
DMF ha sido utilizado como disolvente en las industrias de perfumería y farmacéutica durante mucho tiempo.
en ingles
DMF has been employed as a solvent in the perfume and pharmaceutical sectors for an extended period of time.
DMF exhibits good performances for direct-injection, spark-ignition type engines
DMF shows strong performance in direct-injection, spark-ignition engines.

The nature of the solvent has an important influence on the properties of Pd-based catalysts. The most commonly used solvent for the synthesis of DMF from HMF is THF. Water, alcohols, ionic liquids, and dioxane have also been tried. The low solubility of H2 in EMIMCl (an ionic liquid) was proposed to be responsible for the low conversion of HMF over Pd/C (Table 45, entry 2). (504) Solvation influences the adsorption behavior of the substrates and intermediates. In nonpolar solvents, polar reactants tend to be less strongly solvated and thus adsorb more strongly onto the surface of the catalyst. (542−544) As BHMF is a more polar compound than DMF, less polar solvents such as THF and 1,4-dioxane favor its strong adsorption and a higher selectivity in DMF is obtained than in more polar solvents such as n-butanol.

The choice of solvent significantly affects the properties of Pd-based catalysts. THF is the most commonly used solvent for synthesizing DMF from HMF, although other solvents like water, alcohols, ionic liquids, and dioxane have been attempted. A study suggested that the low solubility of H2 in EMIMCl (an ionic liquid) may be responsible for the decreased conversion of HMF over Pd/C. Solvation plays a role in the adsorption behavior of substrates and intermediates. In nonpolar solvents, polar reactants are less strongly solvated and tend to adsorb more strongly to the catalyst surface. BHMF, being more polar than DMF, shows higher selectivity in DMF when less polar solvents like THF and 1,4-dioxane are used, compared to more polar solvents like n-butanol.

Unfortunately, undesired reactions (e.g., decarbonylation, ring hydrogenation, and ring-opening reactions) as well as catalyst deactivation (expecially for non-noble metal-based catalysts) are unavoidable. The main concerns about the DMF synthesis thus lies in either (i) the improvement of the catalyst selectivity and stability at high reaction temperatures (120–240 °C in most cases) or (ii) the use of moderate reaction conditions.

Regrettably, unwanted reactions such as decarbonylation, ring hydrogenation, and ring-opening reactions, along with catalyst deactivation (especially for non-noble metal-based catalysts), are inevitable. The primary challenges in DMF synthesis focus on either (i) enhancing catalyst selectivity and stability at elevated reaction temperatures (typically 120–240 °C) or (ii) employing milder reaction conditions.

Recently, the hydrogenation reaction of HMF in water under acidic conditions have attracted increasing attention because it affords the synthesis of interesting products through hydrolytic ring-opening and rearrangement. Under these conditions, chain compounds such as the diketone derivative 1-hydroxyhexane-2,5-dione (HHD) [ , , , ] and its hydrogenation product, the linear polyol 1,2,5-HT [ ,  ], or the ring-rearranged product 3-hydroxymethylcyclopentanone (HCPN) [ , , , , ] and its hydrogenated compound, 3-hydroxymethylcyclopentanol [23,27, ] (HCPO), may be obtained.

In recent times, there has been a growing interest in the hydrogenation of HMF in water under acidic conditions as it enables the production of intriguing compounds through hydrolytic ring-opening and rearrangement processes. This reaction can lead to the formation of chain compounds like the diketone derivative 1-hydroxyhexane-2,5-dione (HHD) and its hydrogenation product 1,2,5-HT, or the ring-rearranged product 3-hydroxymethylcyclopentanone (HCPN) and its hydrogenated counterpart 3-hydroxymethylcyclopentanol (HCPO).
the obtention of the HCPN ring-rearranged product from HMF has attracted growing interest lately since first reported in 2014 using a Au/Nb2O5 catalyst
The production of the ring-rearranged product HCPN from HMF has garnered increasing attention recently, especially since its initial report in 2014 using an Au/Nb2O5 catalyst.
In addition to being quite expensive, the synthesis of adipic acid involves huge waste streams
Apart from being costly, the production of adipic acid results in significant waste streams.
Although this technique does not allow to discriminate between Brønsted and Lewis sites, the amount of desorbed NH3 is an indirect measure of the material overall acidity
While this method doesn't differentiate between Brønsted and Lewis sites, the quantity of desorbed NH3 serves as an indirect indicator of the material's overall acidity.

this increase in acidity is mainly observed in the moderate-high temperature region of the NH3-TPD profiles, indicating the presence of medium to strong acid sites in the Ni-supported catalysts, in agreement with other author’s observations

This rise in acidity is primarily noticeable in the moderate to high-temperature range of the NH3-TPD profiles, suggesting the existence of medium to strong acid sites in the Ni-supported catalysts, consistent with findings from other authors.
